EIFS Contractor in Burnet County, TX

Star Stucco of Austin serves Burnet County with professional EIFS installation and repair. Properties around Marble Falls, Burnet, and the Highland Lakes region benefit from Exterior Insulation and Finish Systems that reduce energy costs and provide beautiful, durable exterior surfaces suited to the Hill Country landscape.

Get a Free EstimateCall (512) 706-9699

EIFS Tailored for Burnet County's Hill Country Setting

Burnet County occupies a striking section of the Texas Hill Country where granite outcroppings, oak-studded hillsides, and the Highland Lakes create a landscape that demands exterior finishes with both beauty and resilience. The rocky terrain and variable elevations expose buildings to intense sun on south-facing slopes and windswept conditions along the ridgelines. EIFS provides a lightweight cladding solution that insulates effectively without adding excessive load to structures built on the shallow soils and bedrock common throughout the county.

The communities around Marble Falls and Horseshoe Bay have experienced steady growth, with custom homes, boutique commercial developments, and lakefront properties driving demand for exterior systems that look sophisticated and perform under stress. EIFS delivers smooth or textured finishes in virtually any color, giving architects and homeowners the creative freedom to complement the natural stone and cedar aesthetics prevalent in this region.

Our team has installed EIFS on properties ranging from hillside residences overlooking Lake LBJ to commercial buildings in downtown Burnet. We understand the wind exposure, UV intensity, and substrate conditions that shape product selection and installation methods in this part of the Hill Country.

What's Included With Our EIFS Contractor in Burnet County

Every project includes expert craftsmanship, quality materials, and a commitment to lasting results.

EIFS System Evaluation

We assess your property's wall substrate, orientation, and exposure to determine the best EIFS system for Burnet County's terrain and weather patterns.

Insulation Board Installation

EPS boards are bonded and fastened to the substrate, providing continuous insulation that reduces heat gain on the sun-exposed elevations common in the Hill Country's rolling topography.

Base Coat & Mesh Application

A reinforced base coat with fiberglass mesh is applied over the insulation, creating a resilient shell that withstands the wind and occasional hail events that affect the Burnet County area.

Finish Coat & Texturing

The acrylic finish coat is applied in a color and texture that complements your property, whether it sits among the cedars near Inks Lake or in a Marble Falls neighborhood with a more contemporary character.

Sealant & Flashing Details

Window perimeters, roof transitions, and all penetrations are sealed and flashed to prevent moisture from entering the wall assembly, particularly important given the driving rain events that sweep through the Highland Lakes.

Final Inspection & Walkthrough

Every section of the installation is inspected for proper adhesion, drainage performance, and finish consistency before we walk you through long-term care for the Burnet County climate.

Common EIFS Contractor Needs in Burnet County

From lakeside homes to commercial properties in town, Burnet County property owners use EIFS for a variety of building projects. These are our most common requests.

EIFS Installation on New Construction

Custom home builders around Marble Falls, Horseshoe Bay, and Bertram choose EIFS to achieve distinctive exterior finishes with built-in insulation. We coordinate with builders during framing to integrate drainage planes and attachments before the EIFS goes on.

EIFS Repair & Patching

Woodpecker damage, wind-driven debris, and minor settlement cracks are common repair triggers in Burnet County. We match the existing finish color and texture so repairs blend invisibly into the original wall surface.

Moisture Intrusion Remediation

Properties near the lakes and in low-lying areas of Burnet County sometimes experience moisture intrusion behind older EIFS installations. We open affected sections, remediate substrate damage, install proper drainage layers, and rebuild the system to current specifications.

Foam Trim & Architectural Details

Decorative foam elements such as window casings, arched surrounds, and cornice profiles add Hill Country charm to Burnet County homes without the weight and cost of carved limestone. We shape and install these details as part of the EIFS system.

Commercial EIFS Systems

Hotels, restaurants, and retail spaces in the Marble Falls commercial district use EIFS to create attractive storefronts that meet energy codes. We handle commercial-scale work with attention to business hours and public access requirements.

EIFS-to-Stucco Conversion

Some Burnet County homeowners prefer to replace an aging EIFS installation with traditional hard-coat stucco or a fully updated drainage EIFS. We manage the removal, substrate assessment, and re-cladding as a complete turnkey project.

Why Choose Us for EIFS Contractor in Burnet County?

  • Certified applicators for Sto Corp, Dryvit, and Senergy/BASF with the credentials to deliver manufacturer-backed warranties
  • Hands-on experience with the rocky terrain, high wind exposure, and UV intensity unique to the Burnet County Hill Country
  • Knowledge of energy-efficient building strategies that lower cooling costs for properties in this sun-drenched region
  • Skilled at both boutique residential EIFS projects and commercial installations in Marble Falls and Burnet
  • Full warranty coverage on materials and workmanship when installed by our certified team
  • Upfront pricing and detailed proposals that reflect the actual scope of your project, with no hidden costs

Licensed & Insured

Full liability coverage and workers' compensation for every project in Burnet County.

20+ Years Experience

Decades of hands-on stucco work across Central Texas residential and commercial properties.

Certified Applicators

Certified by Sto Corp, Senergy/BASF, Master Wall, Dryvit, and Parex LaHabra for the highest quality results.

Our EIFS Contractor Process

A straightforward approach from start to finish — no surprises, no shortcuts.

1

Arrange Your EIFS Consultation

Call (512) 706-9699 to schedule a visit to your Burnet County property. We will discuss your vision, inspect the structure, and recommend EIFS solutions that suit both your design preferences and the local environment.

2

System Evaluation & Design

Our team evaluates the substrate, considers exposure factors like wind and sun angle, and selects the EIFS components — insulation type, base coat system, and finish — that will deliver lasting performance on your Hill Country property.

3

Review Scope & Investment

We present a written proposal with a complete breakdown of work, materials, and cost. Every detail is explained so you can make a confident decision before installation begins.

4

Professional Installation

Our certified installers complete the project to manufacturer standards, keep the site orderly, and provide regular updates. A final walkthrough confirms the system meets our quality benchmarks and your expectations.
